about.description: Meet Andrés Santana, Senior Frontend Engineer from Colombia

intro.p1: Most developers build features. I build revenue.
intro.p2: For 4+ years, I've been the frontend lead at Mexico's fastest-growing digital pharmacy. When I joined, the site took 5.8 seconds to load. Users bounced. Sales stalled. I fixed that—cutting load time by 56% and driving a 23% conversion lift.
intro.p3: Today, the platform I architected processes $2M+ monthly, serves 50,000+ patients, and maintains 99.9% uptime. I led the Next.js 15 migration with zero downtime while the team shipped features in parallel.
intro.p4: I work best with teams who care about speed, conversions, and code that scales. If that's you, let's talk.
intro.title: Introduction

about.label: About

aws.description: Deploying and scaling frontend infrastructure on AWS. Experience with S3 + CloudFront for static assets, Lambda@Edge for dynamic rendering, and Amplify for full-stack deployments with CI/CD integration.
aws.title: AWS

cicd.description: Automated pipelines with GitHub Actions, Vercel, and AWS. Feature branch previews, automated testing gates, Lighthouse CI budgets, and zero-downtime deployments for production applications.
cicd.title: CI/CD & DevOps

graphql.description: Designing and consuming GraphQL APIs with Apollo Client and codegen for fully typed queries. Real-time subscriptions for live data feeds, optimistic UI updates, and efficient cache management for complex financial data.
graphql.title: GraphQL

performance.description: Systematic approach to Core Web Vitals: code-splitting, lazy loading, image optimization (WebP/AVIF), critical CSS inlining, and caching strategies. Proven track record reducing LCP by 56% on high-traffic platforms.
performance.title: Performance Optimization

react.description: Building production applications with React 18+ and Next.js App Router. Experienced with Server Components, Streaming, Parallel Routes, and ISR for high-performance, SEO-optimized frontends.
react.title: React / Next.js

tailwind.description: Utility-first styling with Tailwind CSS for rapid, consistent UI development. Custom design system tokens, responsive layouts, and component-level theming integrated with design tools like Figma.
tailwind.title: Tailwind CSS

typescript.description: Strict TypeScript across the full stack. Type-safe API contracts, generic utility types, and end-to-end type safety from database schemas to UI components. Reduces runtime errors and accelerates team velocity.
typescript.title: TypeScript

xstate.description: Modeling complex UI flows with finite state machines using XState. Ideal for multi-step checkout processes, payment flows, and approval workflows where predictable state transitions are critical.
xstate.title: State Management (XState)

about.title: About me

achievements.1: Delivered 10+ projects for startups and SMBs across e-commerce, SaaS, and real estate industries.
achievements.2: Built end-to-end web platforms from Figma designs to production deployment on Vercel.

achievements.1: Led frontend architecture for Mexico's fastest-growing digital pharmacy, serving 50,000+ patients with chronic conditions.
achievements.2: Reduced LCP from 5.8s to 2.5s (-56%), directly improving conversion rates by 23%.
achievements.3: Architected modular checkout system processing $2M+ monthly in transactions with 99.9% uptime.
achievements.4: Led migration from legacy React to Next.js 15 with zero downtime, reducing bundle size by 40%.

1.description: You use a computer all day — spreadsheets, documents, email, reports — and suspect half of it could be automated, but don't know where to start.
1.title: Non-technical professionals

2.description: You've tried ChatGPT a few times, got mediocre results, and concluded 'it's not for my job'. It is — you just need working workflows, not prompts from Twitter.
2.title: Curious but burned

3.description: You lead a team and feel the pressure to 'do something with AI' without hiring engineers or buying enterprise tools you won't use.
3.title: Team leads & managers

1.answer: No. This program is designed for people who don't write code. Everything is built with tools you already know how to click through.
1.question: Do I need to know how to code?

2.answer: The first cohort is in validation. Joining the waitlist costs nothing, gets you first access, and early members get launch pricing.
2.question: When does it start and how much does it cost?

3.answer: Concrete workflows applied to YOUR work: automating repetitive tasks, producing content faster, and a repeatable system to evaluate new AI tools without drowning in hype.
3.question: What will I walk away with?

cohort.description: Live, hands-on sessions in small groups. You leave each session with something working, not just notes.
cohort.title: Live cohort

community.description: A space to share what works, ask questions and keep up as tools change — because they will.
community.title: Community

templates.description: Ready-to-use workflows and templates: video → automation → publishing, tested end-to-end in real work.
templates.title: Templates & workflows

meta.description: A hands-on AI program for non-technical professionals: apply AI to your real work, recover time, and stop fearing obsolescence. Join the waitlist.
meta.title: AI so you don't fall behind | Program for non-technical professionals

problem.p1: Every week there's a new AI tool 'you can't miss', another thread promising you'll be left behind if you don't master it today. The noise is real — and so is the fear.
problem.p2: The truth: you don't need to learn 50 tools or become an engineer. You need a handful of working flows applied to your actual job, and the criteria to ignore the rest.
problem.title: The fear is real. The noise doesn't help.

teaser.description: A hands-on program for non-technical professionals who want to apply AI to their real work — and stop fearing they'll fall behind.
teaser.title: AI so you don't fall behind

waitlist.description: No spam. One email when the first cohort opens — waitlist members get first access and launch pricing.
waitlist.title: Be first in line

whyMe.p1: I build end-to-end AI workflows for a living — video to automation to publishing — and I'm the person my colleagues come to when they need AI translated into something they can actually execute.
whyMe.p2: I also know the fear of being left behind first-hand: I left music behind for stability in tech. That decision taught me to teach from empathy, not from technical arrogance.
whyMe.p3: This program is what I wish had existed then: practical, honest about what works, and built for people whose job is not technology.
whyMe.title: Why learn this with me

project.description: Fixed scope, fixed price. Ideal for well-defined builds: landing pages, dashboards, e-commerce features.
project.title: Project-based

retainer.description: Ongoing development and support with guaranteed availability. Best value for continuous product work.
retainer.title: Monthly Retainer

1.answer: I specialize in React, Next.js and TypeScript frontends—especially e-commerce, fintech and healthcare platforms where performance and conversions matter.
1.question: What kind of projects do you take on?

2.answer: Yes. I've worked remotely with teams in Mexico, Colombia and the US for 5+ years, in English and Spanish, and I'm comfortable across time zones.
2.question: Do you work with international teams?

3.answer: A landing page typically takes 1-2 weeks; a full e-commerce build or migration, 6-12 weeks. After the discovery call you get a written proposal with a concrete timeline.
3.question: How long does a typical project take?

4.answer: Both. I can own the frontend end-to-end or integrate with your existing engineering team, including mentoring and code reviews.
4.question: Do you work solo or with teams?

5.answer: Project-based work is a fixed price agreed upfront. Retainers and consulting are billed monthly or by the hour. Every engagement starts with a free discovery call.
5.question: How does pricing work?

hero.intro: I help companies ship fast, reliable frontends that convert. Pick the engagement that fits—or book a call and we'll figure it out together.
hero.title: Work With Me
